items.patternLabel: Integrator must convert outline-style draft into article prose
items.pattern: Integrator must convert outline-style draft into article prose
items.recommendedAction: Rewrite the integrated draft into a finished article for government and implementation stakeholders. Preserve the current section logic, but convert each bullet-based section into complete explanatory paragraphs written as final audience-facing prose. Remove all meta-outline language such as 'the article should', 'should connect', and 'visuals can', and replace it with direct explanation of how rural water service performance monitoring works as a decision and accountability system. Add at least two explicit visual support elements inside the markdown with clear labels and concise descriptions, for example: 'Visual 1: Performance monitoring flow from data collection to corrective action' and 'Visual 2: Accountability map across village, block, district, and implementing agency levels.' Include a short practical scenario showing how one indicator trigger, such as repeated supply interruption or unresolved repair delay, moves through review, escalation, and follow-up. End with a fully written conclusion that synthesizes indicators, monitoring flow, action triggers, and accountability into Drishvara’s communication model.
tags: topic:how drishvara should explain rural water service performance monitoring with visual clarity
contentType:article
audience:government and implementation stakeholders
